rationale: A is closer to the likely 2.2.0 engineering anchors: it names the coordinator-view class and the most relevant runtime tests for filesystem/jobmanager storage. B is more version-pinned on docs, but its doc anchors look unverified and its test selection is less adjacent to the coordinator-view contract. Neither is perfect; A is less risky and more actionable.

A: Docs are only the release root, not the specific 2.2.0 checkpointing/state-backend pages.
It omits `CheckpointStorageWorkerView` and `CheckpointStorageAccess`, which are closer contract neighbors.
B: The `#part-0001/#part-0016` doc anchors look unverified and may not exist on the cited pages.
`MemoryCheckpointStorageAccessTest` is not an obvious neighboring test for `CheckpointStorageCoordinatorView`.
It misses the more directly relevant `FileSystemCheckpointStorageTest` and `JobManagerCheckpointStorageTest`.
